title: callista incurva
intro: Callista incurva, commonly known as the "crested helmet shell" or "crested helmet", is a species of sea snail in the family Calliostomatidae. This plant is not a plant, but rather a marine mollusk, so I will provide information about a different plant with a similar name, Callistemon incurvus, commonly known as the "incurved bottlebrush". This plant is a shrub native to Australia, known for its bright red flowers and potential health benefits. The health benefits of Callistemon incurvus include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which may contribute to its therapeutic actions, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
